_publications._publicationTitle: Biochemical and structural analyses reveal that the tumor suppressor neurofibromin (NF1) forms a high-affinity dimer.

_contextSnippet: constructs containing epitope-tagged neurofibromin domains were transfected into serum-starved HEK293 cells lacking endogenous NF1, and levels of RAS–GTP after EGF stimulation were measured
details: Used to measure RAS-GTP levels after EGF stimulation, showing full-length NF1 or ABC+DEF co-transfection produced considerably higher stimulation of RAS GTPase activity compared to individual domains
